sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SK Telecom to unveil call recording service for iPhones
feed.summary: SK Telecom plans to introduce a call recording service for iPhone users through its AI app, A dot, coinciding with the iPhone 15 launch in Korea. This will address a long-standing disadvantage for iPhone users in Korea, as Samsung Galaxy phones already offer this feature natively. The company also intends to bring call summary functions to iOS users, which are currently available for Android.

feed.title: Everything we know so far about Project Mugen
feed.summary: Project Mugen, an urban open-world RPG developed by NetEase, has generated significant buzz since its trailer release on August 24th, 2023. The game features an anime-inspired art style, a futuristic setting where humans and the supernatural coexist, team-based combat with unique characters, and multiple traversal options like parkour and wall climbing. While pre-registrations are open, a definitive release date for PlayStation, PC, Android, and iOS platforms has not yet been announced.

feed.title: Why U.S. Bancorp is investing in the static point of sale
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp's Elavon unit is investing in dedicated payment terminal technology through its talech Terminal system, despite the rise of softPOS solutions that turn smartphones into payment terminals. Elavon believes there's a significant demand for terminals that offer comprehensive business management tasks alongside payment acceptance, minimizing IT complexity and upfront costs for small businesses. While supporting softPOS, Elavon argues that dedicated devices provide greater control over software, offer more value-added services, and better cater to certain merchant needs, including longer battery life and a streamlined user interface.

feed.title: iShares MSCI ACWI ETF
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI) offers investors global equity exposure to large and mid-capitalization developed and emerging markets. It tracks the MSCI All Country World Index, providing diversification across numerous countries and sectors efficiently. The ETF is marketed as a low-cost investment vehicle with potential for long-term growth, though it acknowledges inherent investment risks.

feed.title: Expedia Group builds generative AI into multiple products in its “Fall Release”
feed.summary: Expedia Group is unveiling its "Fall Release 2023," introducing numerous features and products for travelers and supplier partners, heavily integrating generative AI. Key updates include AI-powered conversational capabilities within its apps, dynamic travel guides, and "EG Labs" for consumer feedback on new tech. The release also brings influencer content, group trip planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations for hotel partners to enhance performance and guest insights.

feed.title: Calix taps Jabil as part of $42B+ broadband manufacturing plan
feed.summary: Jabil, a St. Petersburg-based tech company, is partnering with California-based Calix Inc. to manufacture parts for expanding broadband internet service in rural America. This collaboration is part of the Biden Administration's $42.45 billion Build America, Buy America (BABA) effort for the Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment (BEAD) program. Jabil will produce Calix’s optical network terminals (ONTs) and optical line terminals (OLTs) in Michigan, creating over 100 new jobs.

feed.title: T-Mobile CEO Sievert recalls the first iPhone launch on T-Mobile
feed.summary: T-Mobile CEO Mike Sievert reflects on the company's transformation from a struggling "distant fourth" carrier that didn't offer iPhones to a leading provider. The turning point was the introduction of the iPhone 5 in 2013 and the simultaneous launch of its 4G LTE network, coupled with innovative contract-free payment plans. Sievert also credits a $3 billion breakup fee from a failed AT&T merger for helping to fund T-Mobile's growth into the "Un-carrier" it is today.

feed.title: AB US Large Cap Strategic Equities ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the AB US Large Cap Strategic Equities ETF (LRGC), including its price, holdings, expense ratio, and performance metrics. It details the fund's top holdings, concentration analysis, ESG scores, and comparisons to peer ETFs, offering a detailed guide for potential investors.

feed.title: Uniqlo picks Tacoma Mall for its next Puget Sound-area store
feed.summary: Uniqlo plans to open a new store in Tacoma Mall, its latest expansion in the Puget Sound area. The Japanese retailer will occupy the space previously held by White House Black Market, directly across from the Apple Store. This move follows other recent Uniqlo openings and plans in the region, including in downtown Seattle and Bellevue.

feed.title: Buy the Dip in Apple, or Has the Trend Turned Rotten?
feed.summary: This article discusses whether investors should "buy the dip" in Apple (AAPL) given its recent revenue decline for three consecutive quarters and concerns about its position in the AI race. It highlights Direxion's leveraged and inverse single-stock ETFs for AAPL (AAPD and AAPU) and broader technology ETFs (TECL and TECS) for traders with varying risk appetites and market outlooks. The article also provides important disclaimers regarding the risks associated with leveraged and inverse ETFs.

feed.title: Should Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) Be on Your Investing Radar?
feed.summary: This article evaluates the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) as a potential investment, highlighting its focus on large-cap growth stocks, low expense ratio, and strong performance. It details VONG's sector exposure, top holdings, and risk profile, while also suggesting alternative ETFs for consideration.

feed.title: FNGG ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FNGG
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the Direxion Daily NYSE FANG+ Bull 2X Shares (FNGG) ETF, detailing its key characteristics such as assets under management, dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings. FNGG offers 200% leveraged exposure to the NYSE FANG+ Index, focusing on big technology and innovative companies. The report also covers its recent performance, dividend history, and answers frequently asked questions about the fund.

feed.title: Qualcomm defeats consumers' antitrust claims over chip supply contracts
feed.summary: Qualcomm has successfully defeated a consumer antitrust lawsuit in California federal court, which alleged that the chipmaker's contracts with device manufacturers artificially inflated mobile phone costs. U.S. District Judge Jacqueline Scott Corley ruled in favor of Qualcomm, stating that allowing the plaintiffs to revive past evidence would lead to prolonged and expensive "do-over litigation." This ruling follows Qualcomm's 2020 victory against a similar lawsuit brought by the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.

feed.title: Apple is ordered to face Apple Pay antitrust lawsuit
feed.summary: Apple has been ordered to face a private antitrust lawsuit brought by payment card issuers. The lawsuit alleges that Apple exerts a 100% monopoly over the domestic market for tap-and-pay wallets on its devices, forcing banks to pay excessive fees and hindering competition and innovation. A U.S. District Judge dismissed a "tying" claim but ruled that plaintiffs could proceed with their claim that Apple violated federal antitrust law.

feed.title: Silicon Hegemon: Could China Take Over Taiwan’s Semiconductor Industry Without Invading?
feed.summary: This article explores the high-impact, low-probability scenario of China taking control of Taiwan's semiconductor industry, particularly TSMC, without a full-scale military invasion. It outlines hypothetical coercion tactics short of war, such as blockades and economic pressure, that could force Taiwan into ceding control. The authors propose military, regulatory, and policy responses for Taiwan and its allies to prevent such an outcome, emphasizing deterrence and preparedness to defend free passage to and from Taiwan.

feed.title: Nvidia's French offices raided in cloud-computing competition inquiry, Wall Street Journal reports
feed.summary: French competition authorities raided Nvidia's offices on suspicion of anticompetitive practices related to cloud computing and graphics cards. The investigation, part of a broader inquiry into potential market dominance in cloud computing, follows Nvidia's significant growth partly due to rising demand for its AI chips. This action aligns with France's recent aggressive stance against large tech companies.

feed.title: Dutch regulator rejects Apple’s objections to fines
feed.summary: The Dutch competition watchdog ACM has rejected Apple's objections to 50 million euros ($53 million) in fines. The fines were imposed because Apple failed to fully comply with orders to allow alternative payment methods for dating apps in the Netherlands. Apple plans to appeal the decision to the Netherlands courts, maintaining that the original order degrades investment incentives and compromises user privacy and data security.

feed.title: What the Cheesecake Factory Effect means for malls
feed.summary: A new paper from Moody's Analytics identifies the "cheesecake factor," suggesting that the presence of a Cheesecake Factory restaurant indicates a mall's financial health. Approximately 93% of loans backed by malls with a Cheesecake Factory are current on payments, compared to 72% for malls without one. This phenomenon highlights how specific anchor stores can serve as unofficial gauges of a mall's economic prospects.

feed.title: JPMorgan Hedged Equity Laddered Overlay ETF
feed.summary: This page provides a detailed profile of the JPMorgan Hedged Equity Laddered Overlay ETF (HELO), including its stock price, dividend information, expense ratio, holdings, and performance metrics. HELO is categorized as a Large Cap Blend Equities ETF and launched on September 28, 2023, with an expense ratio of 0.50%. The fund's top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, and it has accumulated $3,994.0 million in assets under management.

feed.title: AAPU ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:AAPU
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the Direxion Daily AAPL Bull 2X ETF (AAPU), detailing its structure, investment strategy, and performance metrics. It highlights AAPU as a short-term tactical tool designed to deliver 2x the daily price return of Apple stock, emphasizing its volatility due to lack of diversification and the need for frequent rebalancing for sustained leverage. The analysis also covers key financial data such as AUM, dividend yield, expense ratio, and recent price and NAV performance.

feed.title: Scheels opens 250,000-sq.-ft. store in Phoenix
feed.summary: Scheels has opened a new 250,000-sq.-ft. store in Macerich’s Chandler Fashion Center in Phoenix, Arizona, nearly doubling the tenant's previous footprint. This entertainment-based sporting goods store, which features attractions like a Ferris wheel, saltwater aquarium, and wildlife mountain, significantly boosted mall traffic by over 108% on its opening day. The new location is expected to generate four times the revenue of the former tenant, Nordstrom's, and builds on Scheels' strategy of creating destination retail experiences.

feed.title: PayPal Makes Good on Its Apple Pay Card Vow
feed.summary: PayPal has fulfilled its promise to integrate its PayPal and Venmo credit and debit cards with Apple Wallet, nearly a year after the initial vow. This integration allows users to add their cards to Apple Pay for in-app and Safari browser transactions. Former CEO Dan Schulman stated that this move aims to offer more choice to PayPal's merchants and increase branded transactions, citing a similar success with Google Pay in Germany.

feed.title: Blackberry to Split in Two: Why We Like the Idea
feed.summary: BlackBerry announced its intention to split into two distinct businesses: IoT and cybersecurity. Morningstar analysts view this separation positively, especially the IoT business which includes the QNX software for autonomous vehicles, as it is considered the most valuable part of the firm and an attractive investment. The cybersecurity segment, while struggling, is also expected to benefit from better execution as a separate entity and could be an attractive buyout candidate.

feed.title: Tech spared as bond selloff buffets stocks
feed.summary: Treasury yields surged to their highest levels in over a decade as traders anticipated prolonged high borrowing costs from the Federal Reserve, impacting most US stocks. However, the Nasdaq 100, driven by major technology firms like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, saw gains. This market movement occurred despite a last-minute deal to avert a government shutdown, highlighting the market's primary focus on interest rate policy.

feed.title: Moat Investing Rises Above Magnificent Seven Dominance
feed.summary: The article discusses how Morningstar's Moat Index outperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date despite being underweight the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap stocks. It highlights the growing concentration risk posed by these seven technology giants and explains how equal-weighted Moat strategies offer a solution through diversification and attractive valuations. The article also details the September index reconstitutions for both the Moat and SMID Moat Indexes, noting shifts away from growth stocks and into more value-oriented sectors.

feed.title: Google CEO Pichai wrote to founders Page and Brin to decry bad ‘optics’ of search engine deal with Apple when he was in charge of Chrome
feed.summary: Sundar Pichai, years before becoming Google’s CEO, expressed concerns in 2007 emails to co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin regarding the "bad optics" of Google's exclusive search deal with Apple. These emails, used as evidence in a Justice Department antitrust case, revealed Pichai's discomfort with Google being the sole search provider in the Safari browser. The deals, where Google pays Apple and other smartphone makers billions, are central to the government's claim that Google maintains its search dominance through anti-competitive agreements.

feed.title: REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) Price, Holdings, & News
feed.summary: The REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund launched on October 9, 2023. It aims to mirror the returns of the Solactive FANG Innovation Index while generating monthly income by investing in index securities and writing call options. The fund currently has $584.04 million in assets under management and a dividend yield of 27.49%.

feed.title: Qualcomm layoffs in 2023: Why 1,258 jobs are being slashed
feed.summary: Qualcomm is laying off 1,258 employees in California due to a slump in smartphone sales and a prior commitment to cost-cutting. The layoffs, primarily impacting engineering teams in San Diego and Santa Clara, represent 2.5% of its global workforce. The company is trying to diversify into new markets like IoT, VR, and automotive chips, but its revenue continues to be heavily reliant on the struggling smartphone market.

feed.title: IWF ETF: Be Cautious Of High Valuations (NYSEARCA:IWF)
feed.summary: The iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) tracks large-cap U.S. growth stocks and has its largest holdings in Apple and Microsoft. While it generally underperformed some growth ETFs like QQQ, it surpassed others like SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 Growth ETF due to differences in its holdings and sector exposure. The article advises caution due to potentially high valuations.

feed.title: United Promotes Benefits of Bluetooth Enabled Planes
feed.summary: United Airlines is highlighting the benefits of its new Bluetooth-enabled planes, emphasizing how these features, including new AirPods Pro capabilities, improve passenger and crew communication. The airline currently has Bluetooth connectivity on over 100 planes and plans to expand this to about 800 aircraft by 2032 as part of its United Next modernization program. This initiative also integrates other Apple products and aims for significant fuel efficiency improvements.

feed.title: Google joins Adobe, Microsoft in promising to protect its AI users from copyright claims
feed.summary: Google has announced it will legally protect users of its generative AI products against copyright infringement claims. This move follows similar commitments from other tech giants like Adobe and Microsoft, aiming to alleviate concerns about intellectual property violations in AI-generated content. The commitment means Google will shoulder legal responsibility for its users in such cases.

feed.title: Amkor's Largest Global Semiconductor Factory Opens in Yen Phong 2C Industrial Park developed by Viglacera
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Vietnam celebrated the grand opening of its largest global semiconductor factory in the Yen Phong 2C Industrial Park, developed by Viglacera Corporation. This advanced factory, with a $1.6 billion investment, will serve leading electronics companies and is expected to boost Vietnam's semiconductor industry. Viglacera, with over 20 years of experience in industrial real estate, continues to attract significant foreign direct investment, especially in high-tech sectors, and plans further expansion of its industrial park network.

feed.title: Malicious “RedAlert - Rocket Alerts” application targets Israeli phone calls, SMS, and user information
feed.summary: Cloudflare's Cloudforce One Threat Operations Team discovered a malicious Android application impersonating the legitimate "RedAlert - Rocket Alerts" app, widely used in Israel for airstrike alerts. This malicious version, distributed via a deceptive website, collects sensitive user data including call logs, SMS, contacts, and account information, while mimicking the original app's functionality. Users who installed the Android version from `redalerts[.]me` are advised to delete it immediately and reinstall the authentic app from the Google Play Store.

feed.title: Malicious “RedAlert - Rocket Alerts” application targets Israeli phone calls, SMS, and user information
feed.summary: Cloudflare's Cloudforce One Threat Operations Team identified a malicious Android application impersonating the legitimate "RedAlert - Rocket Alerts" app, widely used in Israel. This fake app, distributed via a lookalike website, aimed to steal sensitive user data like contacts, call logs, and SMS messages, exploiting the critical need for rocket alert systems. Users who sideloaded the Android version from the malicious site are advised to delete the app and reinstall the legitimate version from the Play Store.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is largely controlled by institutional shareholders who own 55% of the company
feed.summary: Institutional shareholders control 55% of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), giving them significant influence over the company's share price. The top 25 shareholders own 40% of the company, with The Vanguard Group, Inc. being the largest at 8.3%. Individual investors and public companies hold 39% and 5.9% respectively, while insiders own less than 1%.

feed.title: Weibo trials displaying real names of influencers, starting with its CEO
feed.summary: Weibo is trialling a new policy to display the real names and occupations of influencers, starting with its CEO, Wang Gaofei. The policy initially targets influencers with over 1 million followers and will expand to those with 500,000, affecting fields like politics, finance, and entertainment. This move follows the Cyberspace Administration of China's initiative to regulate "self-media" and has sparked debate regarding its impact on online discourse and user privacy.

feed.title: Apple to spend $1 billion on AI in catch-up move: report
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly investing $1 billion to accelerate its efforts in generative artificial intelligence. This move comes after the company was reportedly "caught off guard" by the rapid rise of AI tools like ChatGPT last year. The investment aims to integrate generative AI technology across all Apple products, with the initiative being led by senior vice presidents John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi.

feed.title: Nvidia and AMD reportedly developing Arm-based PC processors
feed.summary: Nvidia and AMD are reportedly developing new Arm-based PC processors, aiming to enter the Windows computer market as early as 2025. This move aligns with Microsoft's efforts to foster competition and introduce more energy-efficient CPUs with advanced AI features, similar to Apple's Arm-based Macs. Qualcomm currently holds an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft for Arm-based Windows chips, which is set to expire by the end of the year, potentially opening the door for Nvidia and AMD.

feed.title: KT Partners with Thailand’s Jasmine Group for Global AI Collaboration
feed.summary: KT announced a collaboration with Thailand’s Jasmine Group to develop a Thai Large Language Model (LLM) using KT's "Mi:dm" super-scale AI, marking the first overseas venture for a domestically produced super-scale AI. The partnership involves sharing technical expertise for LLM development, establishing a GPU farm, and exploring global LLM business models in Southeast Asia. Both companies aim to enhance Thailand's AI industry competitiveness and expand into other regional markets.

feed.title: It's Not Just Lithium: Tesla, Rivian, And The Green Revolution Rely On These Precious Metals
feed.summary: This article highlights the critical role of precious metals beyond lithium in powering the green revolution, particularly in electric vehicle batteries. It details how cobalt, nickel, silver, and graphite are essential components for companies like Tesla and Rivian, impacting battery performance, energy density, and efficiency. The piece also touches on future trends, such as solid-state batteries, and discusses investment opportunities in the materials driving the EV industry.

feed.title: Nvidia to take on Intel, Qualcomm with Arm-based processors for PC; availability pegged for 2025
feed.summary: Nvidia is developing Arm-based processors for personal computers, aiming to compete with Intel and Qualcomm, with availability expected as soon as 2025. This move intensifies competition in the PC chip market, especially as AMD is also reportedly working on Arm-based processors for PCs. The initiative follows Apple's successful transition to in-house Arm-based chips, boosting Mac sales, and poses a significant challenge to Intel's dominant position.

feed.title: FDA Clears Omnipod 5 App for iPhones
feed.summary: The FDA has granted 510(k) Clearance to the Omnipod 5 app for iPhone, making Insulet Corporation the first to offer a tubeless AID system with full control from compatible Android and iOS smartphones. This clearance follows the Omnipod 5 Automated Insulin Delivery system's initial FDA clearance for patients aged 6 years and older with type 1 diabetes in January 2022, and subsequent approval for children aged 2 years and older. The iOS app will offer the same functionality as the Android version, plus a new custom food feature, with a full market release expected in 2024.

feed.title: ‘Fundamental rethink:’ Investors dump China shares despite new efforts to shore up the economy
feed.summary: Global investors are offloading Chinese shares at an "unprecedented" rate due to concerns over China's economic slowdown, a lack of convincing policy responses, and an opaque business environment. Despite Beijing's efforts to stimulate growth through bond issuance and sovereign wealth fund purchases, these measures have failed to restore investor confidence. Experts suggest that a fundamental rethinking of China investment is underway, driven by a collapse of trust in the Chinese Communist Party and rising US-China tensions.

feed.title: Top 10 Mobile Accessories Companies | Fuelling Market Growth
feed.summary: The global mobile accessories market, valued at USD 295.5 billion in 2022, is projected to reach USD 598.1 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 7.5%. This growth is driven by the increasing adoption of smartphones, technological advancements in mobile devices, and the rising demand for complementary accessories like protective cases, headphones, and smartwatches. The article highlights key players such as Samsung, Apple, Google, Sandisk, Belkin, Harman, Aukey, Sony, Anker, and Bose, providing an overview of their history, products, geographical presence, and recent developments.

feed.title: Apple Looks to Regain Chip Bragging Rights With Trio of M3 Processors
feed.summary: Apple is set to unveil its new M3 chip line, along with updated MacBook Pros and an iMac, at a "Scary Fast"-themed event. This comes as the company also plans a total overhaul of AirPods, increases prices for Apple TV+, and revamps its TV app. The move aims to boost Mac sales and enhance Apple's performance chip offerings.

feed.title: Apple unveils M3, M3 Pro, and M3 Max, the most advanced chips for a personal computer
feed.summary: Apple announced its new M3, M3 Pro, and M3 Max chips, touting them as the most advanced chips for a personal computer. Built on 3-nanometer process technology, these chips introduce a next-generation GPU architecture with features like Dynamic Caching, hardware-accelerated ray tracing, and mesh shading, delivering significant performance improvements over the M1 family of chips. The M3 family also boasts faster CPU performance, an enhanced Neural Engine for AI/ML tasks, and an unrivaled unified memory architecture supporting up to 128GB, along with improved power efficiency for longer battery life and a reduced environmental impact.

feed.title: BlackBerry CEO Chen to retire, director Lynch to hold interim charge
feed.summary: BlackBerry CEO John Chen is set to retire at the end of the week after a decade of leading the company through a strategic pivot from hardware to enterprise software. Richard Lynch, a current board director, will take over as interim CEO while the company searches for a permanent replacement. Chen oversaw BlackBerry's shift to cybersecurity, in-car software, and IoT applications, and initiated plans to separate its IoT and cybersecurity units.

feed.title: 3 ETFs Setting the Gold Standard for Investor Returns
feed.summary: This article highlights three top-performing ETFs for investors seeking wealth building in a bullish market: Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG), Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (QQQ), and VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). These ETFs offer diversification, high liquidity, and lower costs compared to actively managed funds. Each ETF focuses on specific growth sectors like tech and semiconductors, featuring holdings in major companies such as Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Taiwan Semiconductor.

feed.title: MOAT: History May Not Repeat Itself (BATS:MOAT)
feed.summary: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (MOAT) has a strong historical track record and reasonable fees but is significantly underweight in big tech. Given a bullish view on big tech, the author suggests MOAT may underperform the S&P 500 going forward. The article provides an overview of the ETF's strategy and the author's financial disclosures.

feed.title: How To Give Your Passive Investing Approach Some Much-Needed Oomph
feed.summary: This article explores factor investing, also known as "smart beta," as a way to enhance passive investing strategies. It discusses the performance of five popular factors—value, quality, momentum, minimum volatility, and size—highlighting quality as the best performer over the past five years. The piece also suggests considering multifactor ETFs like the iShares US Equity Factor ETF (LRGF) for diversified exposure to these factors.

feed.title: DarioHealth launches smart blood sugar meter for iPhone 15
feed.summary: DarioHealth has launched a new smart blood sugar meter specifically designed for Apple iPhone 15 users. This new device leverages the iPhone 15's USB-C charging port for real-time data capture and instant connectivity, enhancing the personalized DarioHealth management experience. The company aims to innovate and continuously engage members in managing chronic conditions like diabetes.

feed.title: Disney to pay at least $8.6 billion for Comcast’s Hulu stake
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Co. is moving forward with its acquisition of Comcast Corp.’s 33% stake in the streaming service Hulu. Disney expects to pay at least $8.61 billion for the stake, although the final price will be determined through an appraisal process that could extend into 2024, as Comcast believes Hulu is worth considerably more than the initially agreed-upon minimum valuation. This acquisition is a significant step in Disney's streaming strategy, potentially leading to full integration of Hulu's offerings into Disney+.

feed.title: REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive financial overview of the REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI), including its current price, dividend information, expense ratio, and top holdings. It also details the fund's ESG scores, performance metrics, geographic and sector exposure, and technical analysis, offering a detailed profile for potential investors.

feed.title: Apple now has $162 billion in cash on hand, slightly less than last quarter
feed.summary: Apple's cash reserves decreased slightly to $162.1 billion in its fiscal fourth quarter, down from $166.5 billion in the previous quarter. This comes as the company maintained one of the largest cash piles in the U.S. and posted revenue of $89.50 billion, exceeding Wall Street expectations despite analysts predicting a fourth consecutive quarter of revenue decline.

feed.title: Globalstar's new CEO keeps things vague – for now
feed.summary: Globalstar's new CEO, Paul Jacobs, addressed the company's future plans for the first time since taking the helm approximately two months ago. While optimistic about the combination of space and terrestrial assets, Jacobs provided few concrete details, stating he is still reviewing operations and initiatives, including previously announced private wireless network deals. He expressed hope for an agreement within a year for monetizing spectrum assets and highlighted Globalstar's role in direct-to-cell services and IoT, as the company reported increased revenue and raised full-year guidance.

feed.title: Understanding the Role of Chatbots in Virtual Care Delivery
feed.summary: Chatbots are increasingly vital in virtual healthcare delivery, offering applications from administrative tasks like scheduling appointments to clinical uses such as patient triage and mental health support. While they provide significant benefits in efficiency and patient satisfaction, concerns persist regarding their ability to handle complex diagnostic scenarios, data privacy, and HIPAA compliance, requiring ongoing efforts to build trust among both physicians and patients. The article details chatbot categories, their diverse use cases in virtual care, and weighs their benefits against current limitations.

feed.title: 'We're Changing the Structure:' GM's Software Boss Steps Down Amid Major Overhaul
feed.summary: General Motors is reorganizing its software and services engineering team, leading to the departure of former Apple executive Dave Richardson, who was VP of software and services engineering. Sterling Anderson, GM's chief product officer, will now oversee vehicle software engineering and Global Product. This change aims to accelerate the development and delivery of technology experiences as GM prepares to launch a new centralized computer system in 2028 and moves away from phone mirroring in future vehicles.

feed.title: Cyberattack Hits Infosys' McCamish Systems, Disrupting Operations
feed.summary: Infosys' step-down subsidiary, Infosys McCamish Systems, experienced a cyberattack that caused the non-availability of certain applications and systems. The company is collaborating with a cybersecurity products provider and has launched an independent investigation to determine the impact on systems and data. This incident adds to a growing concern about cybersecurity, following other significant breaches and warnings of state-sponsored attacks earlier this year.

feed.title: On-device AI chips: New battleground for chipmakers
feed.summary: Global chipmakers like Samsung Electronics, Qualcomm, Intel, and AMD are intensifying their focus on on-device AI chips for smartphones, laptops, and PCs, recognizing its potential for customized services, lower costs, and enhanced privacy compared to generative AI. This shift is expected to drive significant growth in AI-enabled consumer electronics and contribute to rising memory chip prices. Both on-device AI and generative AI are seen as complementary, with companies like Samsung planning to integrate both into future smart devices.
feed.source: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition
feed.source_domain: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition

feed.title: Starbucks raising wages at least 3% starting Jan. 1
feed.summary: Starbucks announced it will raise wages for its U.S. retail hourly partners by at least 3% starting January 1, 2024, with more tenured employees receiving higher increases up to 5%. This move is part of an expanded benefits package and comes without anticipated menu price increases. The company stated these investments aim to improve the partner experience, reduce turnover, and enhance customer connection scores.

feed.title: Why Warren Buffett has done more to educate investors than any other corporate executive
feed.summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway and a prominent figure on the MarketWatch 50 list, is recognized for his significant influence on investors. He is known for his candid communication, explaining complex business operations, and openly discussing his successes and failures, which has made him an exceptional educator in the investment world.

feed.title: Why Warren Buffett has done more to educate investors than any other corporate executive
feed.summary: Warren Buffett is recognized not only for his success as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway and his strategic investments in companies like Apple and Occidental Petroleum, but also for his exceptional candor with investors. The article highlights his significant role in educating investors about the intricacies of businesses and his willingness to discuss both successes and mistakes. This transparency sets him apart, making his biggest legacy potentially his contribution to investor education.

feed.title: Lionsgate Increases Majority Stake In 3 Arts Entertainment
feed.summary: Lionsgate Entertainment is finalizing a deal to acquire an additional, significant ownership stake in management and production company 3 Arts Entertainment. This move follows Lionsgate's initial majority stake acquisition in 2018 and comes after months of negotiations and a new valuation. The partnership has been fruitful, contributing five recent shows to Lionsgate’s TV series slate, including "Mythic Quest" and "The Serpent Queen."

feed.title: Apple agrees to $25 million settlement with US over hiring of immigrants
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to pay $25 million to settle claims by the U.S. Department of Justice that it illegally favored immigrant workers over U.S. citizens and green card holders for certain jobs. The Justice Department stated that Apple did not recruit U.S. citizens or permanent residents for jobs eligible for a federal program allowing employers to sponsor immigrant workers for green cards. This settlement marks the largest ever for the Justice Department concerning discrimination based on citizenship.

feed.title: Booking.com settles Italian tax dispute with 94-million euro payment
feed.summary: Travel website Booking.com has agreed to pay approximately 94 million euros ($100.25 million) to settle a tax dispute in Italy covering the period from 2013 to 2021. The settlement follows an investigation by Genoa prosecutors into the company's handling of VAT on properties rented through its platform. As part of the agreement, Booking.com also filed its VAT return in Italy for 2022 and committed to acting as a tax substitute for transactions with private individuals not registered for VAT.

feed.title: Qualcomm and Iridium end their satellite chip partnership
feed.summary: Mobile chip giant Qualcomm has terminated its satellite-to-phone partnership with Iridium, less than a year after its announcement. The collaboration, which aimed to provide satellite-to-phone services via Snapdragon Satellite and Iridium's satellites, struggled due to a lack of adoption by smartphone manufacturers who preferred standards-based solutions. Both companies plan to continue exploring standards-based satellite connectivity options, with Iridium now free to engage directly with OEMs and chipmakers.

feed.title: Amazon sued over 'dangerous partnership' with virtual casino apps
feed.summary: Amazon is being sued in a proposed consumer class-action lawsuit for allegedly earning billions through an "illegal internet gambling enterprise." The lawsuit, filed by a Nevada resident, claims Amazon distributed over 30 illegal casino apps and processed payments for virtual chips, maintaining a 30% financial interest. This case is part of broader litigation targeting online social casino apps, with similar lawsuits pending against Apple, Meta, and Google regarding their payment-processing roles.

feed.title: Spotlight on XYLD: An S&P 500 Covered Call Strategy
feed.summary: This article highlights the Global X S&P 500 Covered Call ETF (XYLD) as a strategy for investors looking to reduce volatility and generate income in the current market environment. It explains how XYLD uses a buy/write strategy by selling call options on the S&P 500 Index to produce a competitive yield, which exceeded 11% as of September 2023. The article also suggests that XYLD may be particularly appealing in a sideways-moving market, providing diversification and income for defensive investors.

feed.title: FAANG Gives Way to the ‘Magnificent Seven’
feed.summary: The "FAANG" stock group has evolved into the "Magnificent Seven," with Netflix replaced by Nvidia, and Microsoft and Tesla added to the mix of top U.S. market names. ETF issuers are beginning to launch products specifically targeting these seven stocks, while existing ETFs like the Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (MGK) already hold significant concentrations of them. Investors have various options, from highly concentrated ETFs to diversified funds, to gain exposure to these influential companies.

feed.title: Market Edges: The Recession (And Fourth Turning) Are Here
feed.summary: Hedgeye argues that a recession is currently underway, contrary to investor hopes for a "Santa Claus rally" and standard seasonal trends. They emphasize a data-driven approach ("conditional factoring") over seasonal optimism, providing extensive resources and analysis of dire economic indicators like household debt, credit delinquencies, and retail sector weakness. The article also highlights insights from Demography analyst Neil Howe on "The Fourth Turning" and global recessionary signals from currency and commodity markets.

feed.title: QYLD ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:QYLD
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the Global X NASDAQ 100 Covered Call ETF (QYLD), focusing on its dividends and returns. It highlights QYLD's strategy of generating income by selling covered call options on the Nasdaq-100, resulting in a less volatile return pattern compared to traditional Nasdaq-100 ETFs. The analysis covers key statistics such as AUM, dividend yield, expense ratio, holdings, and historical performance, alongside frequently asked questions about the fund.

feed.title: Spotlight on illegal hiring practices: Apple pays $25M for recruiting, job ad mistakes
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to pay $25 million to settle allegations of illegal hiring practices related to its PERM program. A DOJ investigation found Apple failed to recruit U.S. citizens or green card holders, did not advertise these positions on its external website, and required paper applications for PERM jobs while allowing electronic applications for others. The settlement includes $18.25 million for discrimination victims and $6.75 million for civil penalties, with Apple also agreeing to revise its recruitment practices and provide employee training.

feed.title: FMR LLC Bolsters Portfolio with Fidelity International Value Fac
feed.summary: FMR LLC, also known as Fidelity, has significantly expanded its investment portfolio by acquiring 350,126 shares of the Fidelity International Value Factor ETF (FIVA) at $21.95 per share. This transaction, executed on October 31, 2023, increases FMR LLC's holdings in FIVA to 1,099,261 shares, representing a 32.33% position in its portfolio. The move reflects Fidelity's strategic investment in an ETF that has shown a 9.1% year-to-date performance.

feed.title: FMR LLC Acquires New Stake in Magenta Therapeutics Inc
feed.summary: FMR LLC, through its flagship company Fidelity Investments, has acquired a new stake in Magenta Therapeutics Inc (MGTA) by purchasing 2,218,212 shares at $0.6996 per share. This transaction gives FMR LLC a 14.97% ownership stake in the clinical-stage biotechnology company, indicating a strategic investment aligning with the firm's focus on technology and healthcare sectors. Despite Magenta Therapeutics currently operating at a loss, FMR LLC's move suggests a belief in the company's long-term value and transformative potential in stem cell and gene therapy.

feed.title: FMR LLC Bolsters Position in Fidelity Low Volatility Factor ETF
feed.summary: FMR LLC (Fidelity) has increased its holdings in the Fidelity Low Volatility Factor ETF (FDLO) by acquiring an additional 892,483 shares on October 31, 2023. This strategic move brings their total ownership in FDLO to 19.61%, reflecting confidence in the ETF's strong performance and growth potential. The transaction aligns with FMR LLC's growth-oriented investment philosophy and diversifies their extensive portfolio, which includes major tech holdings like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Tepper’s Appaloosa dumps Apple stake and loads up on Amazon’s stock
feed.summary: David Tepper’s Appaloosa hedge fund significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com Inc. and completely divested its position in Apple Inc. during the last quarter. The fund also raised its holdings in Google parent Alphabet Inc. and Facebook parent Meta Platforms Inc., as detailed in a recent SEC filing. This move indicates a shift in Tepper's portfolio allocation towards these tech giants.

feed.title: What's next for satellite communications on Android?
feed.summary: Apple has extended its Emergency SOS via satellite service for iPhone 14 and 15 users to two years, while Qualcomm's Snapdragon Satellite initiative for Android has failed due to lack of OEM integration. This leaves Iridium, a key satellite provider, free to pursue new partnerships directly with smartphone makers. The future of satellite communication on Android is unclear, though Samsung is rumored to introduce a solution with the Galaxy S24, and current options include standalone devices like the Motorola Defy Sat Link and Garmin inReach Mini 2.

feed.title: Warren Buffet's latest buys and sells
feed.summary: This article details Berkshire Hathaway's latest stock buys and sells from its third-quarter 2023 13F filing, highlighting that the company was a net seller despite a tough market quarter, leading to a record cash stake. It identifies three undervalued Warren Buffett stocks within Berkshire Hathaway’s portfolio—Bank of America, American Express, and Kraft Heinz—providing Morningstar's analysis and key metrics for each. The piece also lists the stocks Berkshire Hathaway sold entirely, including Activision Blizzard, Celanese, General Motors, Johnson & Johnson, Mondelez International, Procter & Gamble, and United Parcel Service, while noting a significant reduction in its Chevron holdings.

feed.title: Unity 6 debuts next year with AI-powered innovations and platform updates
feed.summary: Unity announced its upcoming Unity 6 platform, set to launch in 2024, featuring significant AI innovations and platform updates. Key announcements from the Unite conference include the early access of Unity Muse for content creation, a preview of Unity 6, and the introduction of Unity Cloud. These updates aim to address developer feedback and enhance the game development process following recent controversies over pricing changes.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Has Bought Shares of This Stock for 21 Consecutive Quarters -- and It's Not Apple
feed.summary: Warren Buffett has consistently invested in Berkshire Hathaway's own stock for 21 consecutive quarters, authorizing over $72 billion in repurchases since mid-2018. This strategy, enabled by a change in buyback clauses, aims to reward long-term shareholders by increasing their ownership stakes and potentially boosting earnings per share. While Apple constitutes nearly half of Berkshire's invested assets, the frequent repurchase of Berkshire Hathaway shares itself highlights Buffett's conviction in his own company.

feed.title: AT&T’s Black Friday Deals on the iPhone 15 Pro & More
feed.summary: AT&T announced its Black Friday deals for the holiday season, offering significant savings on various Apple products including the iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15, iPhone 14, iPhone 14 Pro, iPhone SE 3rd Gen, iPad, and Apple Watch. Deals include trade-in options, no trade-in required offers, and low monthly payments, catering to both new and existing customers. Additionally, AT&T highlighted benefits like flexible shopping, unlimited plans with 5G access, AT&T Fiber deals, and special discounts for military, veterans, teachers, nurses, and physicians.

feed.title: Network of Lies review: Brian Stelter on Fox News, Trump and Dominion
feed.summary: Brian Stelter's book "Network of Lies" examines Fox News's role in spreading election conspiracies following the 2020 election, leading to significant legal and financial repercussions, such as the Dominion settlement. The review highlights Rupert Murdoch's recent step down, the Murdochs' balancing act of profitability and audience satisfaction, and the ongoing influence of Donald Trump on the network and Republican politics. Despite the controversies, the article suggests little change is expected from Fox News as it navigates complex defamation lawsuits and competitive political landscapes.

feed.title: Apple’s In-House Technologies Team Still Has Plenty of Work to Do
feed.summary: This article discusses Apple's intensive efforts to design and manufacture its own core hardware components, such as cameras, screens, modems, and batteries, for its products. It also touches on Apple's adoption of the RCS texting standard, the revelation of its revenue share from the Google search deal, and a key health executive's move to Oura. The piece highlights that Apple's in-house technologies team has extensive work ahead in its strategic initiative to control major parts of its devices.

feed.title: Venmo, Cash App users sue Apple over peer-to-peer payment fees
feed.summary: Venmo and Cash App customers have filed a proposed class-action lawsuit against Apple Inc. alleging the company abused its market power in mobile peer-to-peer payments, leading to "rapidly inflating prices." The lawsuit claims Apple's agreements with PayPal's Venmo and Block's Cash App violate U.S. antitrust law by limiting "feature competition," including prohibiting decentralized cryptocurrency technology. This case adds to Apple's ongoing antitrust legal challenges, seeking an injunction that could force Apple to divest or segregate its Apple Cash business.

feed.title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF With the Nasdaq At An All-Time High? History Offers a Clear Answer.
feed.summary: This article discusses the Invesco QQQ ETF and whether it's a good investment despite the Nasdaq Composite being at an all-time high. It analyzes historical data of the Nasdaq's performance after reaching new highs, suggesting that further gains are common. The article concludes that dollar-cost averaging into QQQ is a sensible strategy regardless of market highs, as it tracks the Nasdaq 100 which tends to rise over time.

feed.title: Gaming Industry Charts a Rebound in 2023
feed.summary: The gaming industry is experiencing a significant rebound in 2023 driven by strong console sales, new game releases, and innovations in mobile and cloud gaming. This recovery, following a dip in 2022, is further supported by anticipation of increased M&A activity and strategic moves by major players like Microsoft, Apple, and Netflix, positioning the sector for continued growth into 2024. The article highlights strong U.S. sales, hardware as a primary catalyst, and key factors like M&A and technological advancements shaping the industry's future trajectory.

feed.title: Sonos Readies $400-Plus Headphones to Rival Apple and Bose, TV Set-Top Box
feed.summary: Sonos Inc. is planning a significant expansion into new product categories, including headphones priced over $400 and a TV set-top box. These new offerings are expected to compete with products from Apple, Bose, and Roku, marking a strategic move beyond its traditional smart speakers and sound bars. The headphones could launch as early as April, with the set-top box following by the end of 2024.

feed.title: InterDigital’s Alain Mourad Elected Chair of New ETSI Industry Specification Group on Integrated Sensing and Communications
feed.summary: Dr. Alain Mourad, Head of InterDigital’s Wireless Lab Europe, has been elected Chair of the newly formed ETSI Industry Specification Group (ISG) on Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC). This group aims to establish the technical foundation for ISAC technology and its standardization in 6G, defining use cases and developing evaluation roadmaps. The ISG will also study privacy, security, and sustainability aspects of ISAC within the 6G framework.

feed.title: Department of Justice and Apple Reach $25 Million Landmark Agreement
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice announced a $25 million agreement with Apple Inc. to resolve allegations of discriminatory hiring practices related to the permanent labor certification (PERM) program. Apple's recruitment practices for PERM positions, which included not advertising on its public-facing career website and requiring mailed paper applications, deterred U.S. workers. The settlement includes $6.75 million in civil penalties and $18.25 million for a backpay fund for victims, and mandates Apple to align PERM recruitment with its standard practices.

feed.title: OpenAI Says Sam Altman to Return as CEO in Dramatic Reversal
feed.summary: Sam Altman is set to return as CEO of OpenAI after being ousted less than a week prior, following intense negotiations and pressure from investors like Microsoft and nearly all of the company's employees. The agreement includes a new initial board of directors led by Bret Taylor, with Larry Summers and Adam D’Angelo, and an internal investigation into the conduct that led to Altman's dismissal. This dramatic reversal comes after widespread calls for Altman's reinstatement and highlights the significant influence of investors and employees on the future of the AI startup.

feed.title: Warren Buffett-Led Berkshire Hathaway Sells Its Entire Procter & Gamble Stake. Should You?
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway fully exited its small position in Procter & Gamble, likely due to consolidating its portfolio and P&G's premium valuation compared to other opportunities like Apple. Despite Berkshire's sale, P&G is still considered a worthwhile investment for risk-averse individuals due to its strong brand development, pricing power, and commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks. The article suggests that while P&G's valuation is high, its other characteristics make it a solid choice for passive income and portfolio stability.

feed.title: Sonos is reportedly working on a streaming video TV set top box, along with headphones
feed.summary: Sonos is reportedly expanding its product line beyond speakers and soundbars. The company isSaid to be working on over-the-ear headphones for a March 2024 launch, possibly priced between $400 and $500, and high-end earbuds. Additionally, Sonos is reportedly developing an Android-based streaming video TV set-top box, codenamed "Pinewood," which could launch in late 2024 or early 2025.

feed.title: Deere must face US farmers' 'right-to-repair' lawsuits, judge rules
feed.summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Deere & Co must face consolidated lawsuits from farmers accusing the agricultural machinery maker of unlawfully conspiring to restrict maintenance and repair services. The judge rejected Deere's arguments for dismissal, stating that the plaintiffs met the legal thresholds to pursue their antitrust claims. This case is part of a broader "right-to-repair" movement challenging restrictive repair practices across various industries.

feed.title: Amkor to build $2 billion semiconductor testing plant in Peoria. 2,000 jobs promised
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to build a $2 billion advanced semiconductor packaging and testing facility in Peoria, Arizona, creating approximately 2,000 jobs. The plant will partner with TSMC, testing chips produced for Apple Inc., and is expected to begin production within two to three years. This investment is seen as a significant step in bolstering the U.S. semiconductor supply chain and is supported by the CHIPS and Science Act.

feed.title: Apple announces expanded partnership with Amkor for advanced silicon packaging in the U.S.
feed.summary: Apple announced an expanded partnership with Amkor, designating Apple as the first and largest customer for Amkor's new manufacturing and packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This facility will package Apple silicon produced at the nearby TSMC fab, strengthening Apple's commitment to American manufacturing. Amkor plans to invest approximately $2 billion in the project, creating over 2,000 jobs, as part of Apple's broader commitment to invest $430 billion in the U.S. economy.

feed.title: AI ETFs see huge performance dispersion in 2023
feed.summary: Artificial Intelligence ETFs have shown significant performance dispersion in 2023, with top strategies more than doubling the returns of the lowest performers. This disparity is largely attributed to the heavy weighting of certain ETFs towards US equities and the "magnificent seven" tech stocks. While these mega-cap companies have driven much of the AI theme's returns, there is still potential for pure-play AI companies to join the rally.

feed.title: Amkor Announces U.S. Advanced Packaging and Test Facility In Peoria
feed.summary: Amkor Technology, Inc. announced a $2 billion investment to build an advanced packaging and test facility in Peoria, Arizona, creating 2,000 new jobs. This facility will be the largest outsourced advanced packaging facility in the US, bolstering America's national security and semiconductor supply chain. Apple will be the first and largest customer, and Amkor has applied for CHIPS funding to support the project.

feed.title: $2 billion semiconductor testing and packaging facility planned for Peoria
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to build a $2 billion semiconductor testing and advanced packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona, near the TSMC plant. This facility, expected to create 2,000 jobs, will test and package chips for Apple and aims to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain. The project is seen as a crucial step for Arizona's semiconductor ecosystem and national security.

feed.title: SP Funds S&P Global Technology ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:SPTE)
feed.summary: The SP Funds S&P Global Technology ETF (SPTE) is an exchange-traded fund that invests in global technology companies adhering to Shariah investment principles, weighted by market capitalization. Launched on November 30, 2023, the ETF currently has assets under management of $151.42 million and a net expense ratio of 0.55%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Apple, and NVIDIA.

feed.title: Who says corporate bonds are dull? Apple’s bonds have seen equity-like returns in the past month
feed.summary: Apple Inc. bonds have generated equity-like returns for investors in the past month, with prices climbing as Treasury yields have tumbled. This rally, fueled by hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2024, has led to significant gains in the broader fixed-income market, with the Bloomberg US Aggregate Bond Index seeing its biggest monthly gain since May 1985.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock rose by 0.31% to $189.95 on Thursday, outperforming the mixed stock market session which saw the Dow Jones Industrial Average rise and the NASDAQ Composite Index fall. The company's shares closed $8.28 below their 52-week high.

feed.title: Apple to become largest customer of new $2B chip packaging facility in Arizona
feed.summary: Apple announced it will be the primary customer for Amkor Technology Inc.'s new $2 billion chip packaging facility in Arizona, which will package chips produced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) at a nearby complex. This facility will play a crucial role in assembling and protecting the next generation of processors for various Apple products. The investment underscores Apple's commitment to American manufacturing and will create over 2,000 jobs.

feed.title: Cognizant Technology Solutions to lay off employees tied to Apple in Austin
feed.summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions announced layoffs impacting employees linked to Apple in Austin. The contractors affected will receive pay while exploring other positions within Cognizant. This move adds to a series of technology sector layoffs in Austin, though Central Texas is noted to be somewhat sheltered from broader industry cuts.

feed.title: One ETF that focuses on employee happiness is outperforming the market this year
feed.summary: An ETF focused on companies with high employee happiness, the Harbor Human Capital Factor U.S. Large Cap ETF (HAPI), has outperformed the S&P 500 this year, climbing nearly 25% compared to the benchmark's less than 20% gain. The fund's strategy is based on Irrational Capital's extensive employee surveys and publicly available data, creating a "human capital score" that is considered a novel and uncorrelated investment factor. While the large-cap HAPI fund includes major tech stocks contributing to its performance, a similar small-cap ETF (HAPS) is also available, offering potential for future growth.

feed.title: Walmart stops advertising on X, adding to growing list of companies
feed.summary: Walmart has ceased advertising on X, joining several other major companies like Apple, Disney, and IBM, citing that it found other platforms to better reach its customers. This decision was part of a gradual reduction in advertising on X, not a sudden halt, according to a source familiar with the situation and an X executive. The social media platform has faced a significant exodus of advertisers due to concerns over antisemitic content, exacerbated by recent controversies involving Elon Musk's comments and his subsequent strong reaction to advertisers.

feed.title: Amkor to invest US$2bn in US chip packaging
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to invest US$2 billion to build an advanced semiconductor packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This new plant will create up to 2,000 jobs and is expected to start production within two to three years, primarily packaging chips made for Apple by TSMC in Phoenix. The investment aims to bolster the US’s chipmaking capabilities, reduce reliance on foreign packaging capacity, and has applied for Chips Act funding.

feed.title: Sonos roadmap reveals multiple audio launches intended in 2024
feed.summary: Sonos is planning an "aggressive" roadmap of new audio products and software updates for 2024, including new in-ceiling speakers, headphones, a second-generation Roam, a high-end amplifier, and a high-end soundbar. A set-top box and subwoofer are also planned for late 2024 or 2025 releases. The company aims to revive sales through these launches, despite facing skepticism in competitive markets.

feed.title: This Growth-Focused ETF Makes for an Excellent Long-Term Investment
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) is highlighted as an excellent long-term investment, having seen a 46% gain this year due to its focus on growth stocks. QQQ tracks the NASDAQ-100 Index, which includes 100 of the largest non-financial companies on the NASDAQ, with a heavy concentration in technology giants like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. The ETF offers investors diversified exposure within the tech sector and has historically shown strong performance, more than doubling in value over five years, making it suitable for maximizing returns.

feed.title: Hartford US Quality Growth ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Hartford US Quality Growth ETF (HQGO), which was established on December 5, 2023, by Hartford. The ETF focuses on large-cap equity and tracks a multifactor index of US large-cap stocks with strong growth potential, while also increasing exposure to quality and momentum. It manages $49.91 million in assets under management across 163 holdings, with top positions including NVIDIA Corp, Alphabet Inc, Apple Inc, Amazon.com Inc, and Microsoft Corp.

feed.title: 美股科技股“疯牛”行情未完待续? AI仍是引爆明年行情的关键
feed.summary: Wedbush機構預計美股科技股在2024年將上漲至少20%，主要得益於人工智能（AI）和雲計算支出的大幅增長。分析師將AI比作互聯網的“1995時刻”，預計未來十年AI支出規模將達到1萬億美元，並點名英偉達和微軟將引領這一趨勢。儘管宏觀經濟不確定性存在，Wedbush仍堅定看好科技行業，並預計納斯達克100指數明年在高科技巨頭帶領下將大漲20%以上。

feed.title: Amex CEO talks co-brand partnerships after Apple reports
feed.summary: American Express CEO Steve Squeri discussed co-branded partnerships, emphasizing that Amex's "premium" card designation means it doesn't align with all potential partners, a point highlighted amidst reports of Amex potentially replacing Goldman Sachs as Apple's credit card partner. Squeri noted that Amex seeks partnerships that offer strong value propositions, target premium customers, and provide distribution while yielding "premium economics." Analysts have speculated on several potential suitors for Apple's card partnership, including Amex, JPMorgan Chase, Synchrony Financial, Capital One, and Bread.

feed.title: Why Investors Should Keep An Eye on Small-Cap Stocks
feed.summary: This article discusses how the increasing concentration of market-cap-weighted indexes, particularly driven by large-cap tech stocks, is creating historical levels of concentration. It explores the implications for the size premium, suggesting that higher market concentration leads to increased expected returns for small-cap stocks due to capital misallocation and reduced investor demand for smaller firms, making them an attractive area for investors.

feed.title: CastleArk Enters ETF Market With Large Growth ETF CARK
feed.summary: CastleArk has launched its first exchange-traded fund, the actively managed CastleArk Large Growth ETF (CARK), which targets 25-30 of the largest and most successful growth companies. The ETF charges 54 basis points and replicates CastleArk's successful Large Cap Growth strategy, previously confined to institutional separately managed accounts. Co-Founder Jerome Castellini emphasizes the need for active management in the current market, especially given the concentration of "Magnificent Seven" stocks, to offer a risk-based alternative to indexed large-cap holdings.

feed.title: Who’s ready for a Gamestop mutual fund? Not this broker
feed.summary: Wedbush analysts are critical of GameStop's decision to delegate investment authority to CEO Ryan Cohen, allowing him to invest the company's capital in equities and other investments. Analyst Michael Pachter suggests that if GameStop believes in its own shares, it should use excess cash for buybacks instead. The move is compared to AMC's "embarrassingly stupid" investment in Hycroft Mining, which saw significant losses.

feed.title: Tesla’s Dojo Supercomputer Head Exits in Blow to Efforts
feed.summary: Ganesh Venkataramanan, the head of Tesla's Dojo supercomputer project, has left the company after five years, posing a significant setback to the automaker's self-driving technology endeavors. Peter Bannon, a long-time Tesla director and former Apple executive, has taken over leadership of the project. This departure comes as Tesla plans to invest over $1 billion in Dojo by the end of 2024, a project analysts believe could add $500 billion to Tesla's valuation.

feed.title: TIDAL cuts 10% of staff as parent company Block enforces cost-saving measures
feed.summary: Music streaming service TIDAL has laid off 10% of its workforce, impacting about 40 employees, as part of parent company Block Inc.'s cost-saving measures. This move aligns with Block CEO Jack Dorsey's November letter to investors, stating the company's intent to cap payroll at 12,000 employees. The layoffs at TIDAL reflect a broader trend of staff reductions across the tech and music industries, following similar cuts by companies like Spotify, Amazon, Google, and Meta.

feed.title: CastleArk Enters ETF Market With Large Growth ETF CARK
feed.summary: CastleArk has launched its first exchange-traded fund, the CastleArk Large Growth ETF (CARK), an actively managed ETF focused on large-cap growth companies. The fund targets 25-30 leading growth companies, identifying competitive strength and seeking to optimize risk through quantitative inputs. CastleArk aims to provide an actively managed alternative to index-heavy portfolios, especially given the concentration of "Magnificent Seven" stocks in the large-cap growth sector.

feed.title: Just 3 of the "Magnificent Seven" Have Outperformed the S&P 500 Since 2022. Here's the One I'd Buy for 2024 and 2025.
feed.summary: This article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks against the S&P 500 since 2022. It identifies Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple as the only three that have outperformed the S&P 500. The author argues that Alphabet, despite underperforming recently, is the best buy for 2024 and 2025 due to its valuation, potential in digital advertising, and cloud segment growth.

feed.title: Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:WISE)
feed.summary: The Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF (WISE) tracks an index of companies involved in AI-related industries, with a concentrated portfolio of 89 holdings. Launched on December 8, 2023, the ETF currently has $33.55 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.35%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Advanced Micro Devices, Intel, Apple, and NVIDIA.

feed.title: 'You have to be difficult': How Gap and J.Crew icon Mickey Drexler became the king of mall-brand retail
feed.summary: This article details how Mickey Drexler, a prominent figure in retail, transformed Gap and revitalized J.Crew, known for his unconventional leadership. He also founded Old Navy and Madewell, served on Apple's board, and heavily influenced Alex Mill, a company started by his son. Drexler is characterized by his micromanagement style and significant impact on several successful brands.

feed.title: The Nasdaq-100 Has Had a Monster 2023 So Far. Is It Too Late to Invest in It Before 2024 Arrives?
feed.summary: The Nasdaq-100 has seen a significant rally of 45% in 2023 after a difficult 2022, outperforming other major U.S. indexes. Despite this strong performance, the article suggests that it's not too late to invest, emphasizing a long-term approach and dollar-cost averaging to mitigate short-term volatility. It highlights the Invesco QQQ ETF as a common way to invest in the Nasdaq-100, composed of innovative companies poised for continued growth in industries like AI, EVs, and biotech.

feed.title: Apple supplier Corning to set up Rs 1,000 crore Gorilla Glass facility in Tamil Nadu
feed.summary: Corning Inc., a key Apple supplier and Gorilla Glass manufacturer, plans to invest Rs 1,000 crore in a new manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu, India. This facility, a collaboration with Indian partner Optiemus Infracom, will be located near other Apple suppliers and is expected to start operations in 2024, initially producing 30 million pieces of cover glass for smartphones. The move comes after Corning chose Tamil Nadu over Telangana, seeking central government incentives under the SPECS scheme.

feed.title: How Google’s Epic Legal Defeat Threatens the App-Store Industry’s $200 Billion Business Model
feed.summary: Google's recent legal defeat against Epic Games threatens the $200 billion app store industry, which includes Apple. A jury found Google's app store practices anticompetitive, which could lead to significant changes in commission rates and developer options. This verdict could also impact Apple's similar business model, despite its previous victory against Epic, as regulators and lawmakers globally scrutinize app store rules and consumer sentiment leans against the power of tech giants.

feed.title: Buffett's Berkshire Slashes Losing HP Bet by Nearly 60% in 3 Months
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has significantly reduced its stake in HP by 57% since mid-September, selling off nearly 60% of its holdings and cutting its ownership from over 12% to about 5%. This move comes as HP has been a losing bet for Berkshire, with the firm selling shares below its initial purchase price from early 2022. Berkshire has been actively paring back its stock portfolio recently, accumulating a record cash pile.

feed.title: DGRW Has Hallmarks of a Great Dividend ETF
feed.summary: The WisdomTree US Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) is highlighted as a strong dividend ETF due to its focus on companies' return on assets and return on equity, rather than just yield. This strategy has allowed DGRW to outperform the S&P 500 and other dividend indexes, especially in high-interest-rate environments. The ETF's approach aims for reduced volatility, potential capital appreciation, and diversification, including a significant allocation to tech stocks.

feed.title: The Apple Inc (AAPL) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
feed.summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation for Apple Inc. (AAPL) to determine if the stock is undervalued. Based on the DCF analysis, Apple's stock appears overvalued with a DCF value of $118.68 per share compared to a current market price of $198.08, resulting in a negative margin of safety of -66.91%. The author emphasizes that this is a preliminary analysis and investors should conduct further research.

feed.title: US Chamber, Comcast, tech group split over net neutrality
feed.summary: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce and Comcast Corp are opposing the Federal Communications Commission's proposal to reinstate net neutrality rules, arguing it would harm investment and innovation. Conversely, major tech firms like Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms support the reinstatement to preserve open internet access. The FCC's move to regain regulatory oversight, following Democrats taking majority control, aims to ensure a "fast, open, and fair" internet, while some states also have existing net neutrality laws.
